title: Human-Written SEO Long-Form Article
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a 100% unique, plagiarism-free, SEO-optimized long-form article with outline, meta description, and FAQ.
prompt:
Role: You are an expert SEO copywriter and subject-matter authority.

Task: Write a comprehensive, 100% original, plagiarism-free article about [TOPIC], targeting the primary keyword [KEYWORD] for [informational/commercial] search intent. Naturally place the keyword in the H1, first paragraph, and 2–3 H2s without stuffing. Write in [LANGUAGE] with a [TONE] tone, at an 8th-grade reading level, minimum 1500 words.

Format: Output in this order — (1) SEO title (≤60 chars) and meta description (≤155 chars); (2) H1; (3) hooking intro; (4) H2/H3 sections with short paragraphs and bullets; (5) a 5-question FAQ formatted for FAQ schema; (6) a short conclusion with a CTA. Output only the article.

title: Yoast SEO Green-Light Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Produces an article engineered to pass every Yoast SEO and readability check.
prompt:
Role: You are a WordPress SEO specialist who optimizes for the Yoast SEO plugin.

Task: Write an article on [TOPIC] for the focus keyphrase [KEYWORD]. Hit every Yoast green bullet: keyphrase in the SEO title, slug, meta description, first paragraph, one subheading and image alt text; keyphrase density 0.5–2.5%; 30%+ sentences with transition words; under 25% of sentences over 20 words; a subheading at least every 300 words; Flesch reading ease above 60.

Format: Deliver SEO title, slug, meta description, suggested featured-image alt text, then the full article with H2/H3 markup.

title: Keyword Cluster & Content Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a topic-cluster keyword strategy: pillar page plus supporting articles with intent and funnel stage.
prompt:
Role: You are a senior SEO strategist.

Task: From the seed keyword [KEYWORD] in the [INDUSTRY] niche, build a topic-cluster content plan with 1 pillar page and 8–12 supporting articles that internally link to it.

Format: A markdown table with columns — Cluster | Page Type (Pillar/Supporting) | Working Title | Target Keyword | Search Intent | Funnel Stage (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU) | Suggested Word Count. After the table, list 5 internal-linking recommendations and 3 low-competition long-tail keywords to target first.

title: Outrank a Competitor Article
author: PromptDeck
description: Analyzes a competitor's ranking page and writes a more complete article designed to outrank it.
prompt:
Role: You are an SEO content strategist.

Task: I want to outrank the competitor page [COMPETITOR URL], which targets [KEYWORD]. Infer the topics and questions that page likely covers, identify 5 content gaps they miss, then write a more comprehensive, better-structured article covering everything they do plus the gaps.

Format: Start with an SEO title, meta description, and an outline, then the full draft. Be genuinely more useful, not just longer.

title: Clickable Blog Title & Hook Ideas
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates 15 high-CTR, SEO-aware blog titles plus intro hooks across proven formulas.
prompt:
Role: You are a headline strategist who blends CTR psychology with SEO.

Task: Generate 15 blog title ideas for [TOPIC] targeting [KEYWORD], using a mix of formulas (how-to, listicle, question, mistakes-to-avoid, ultimate guide, contrarian, result-driven). Keep titles under 60 characters where possible and front-load the keyword when natural.

Format: A table — Title | Formula | Why it works. Then write 2 opening-hook variations for the single best title.

title: SEO FAQ Schema Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates a keyword-rich FAQ answering real 'People Also Ask' questions, ready for FAQ schema.
prompt:
Role: You are an SEO content specialist who writes for featured snippets.

Task: Generate an FAQ section for a page about [TOPIC] targeting [KEYWORD]. Produce 8 questions real users search (People Also Ask style), mixing informational and commercial intent, each answered in 2–4 useful sentences with related keywords used naturally.

Format: Output twice — (1) human-readable Q&A; (2) valid FAQPage JSON-LD schema ready to paste.

title: SEO Title Tags & Meta Descriptions (Bulk)
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates optimized title tags and meta descriptions for a list of pages or keywords.
prompt:
Role: You are an on-page SEO specialist.

Task: Write title tags and meta descriptions for these pages/keywords: [LIST PAGES OR KEYWORDS] for the brand [BRAND]. Titles ≤60 chars with the keyword near the front; meta descriptions ≤155 chars, benefit-led, with a soft CTA and the keyword used naturally. Avoid duplicate phrasing across rows.

Format: A table — Page/Keyword | Title Tag | Meta Description.

title: SEO Content Brief for Writers
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a complete SEO brief a writer can follow to produce a rank-ready article.
prompt:
Role: You are an SEO editor briefing a freelance writer.

Task: Build a content brief for an article targeting [KEYWORD] with [SEARCH INTENT] for [AUDIENCE]. Include the angle, target word count, and the questions the piece must answer.

Format: Deliver — Working title options (3); Primary + 5 secondary keywords; Search intent; Suggested H2/H3 outline; Key questions to answer; Internal & external link suggestions; Tone & word count; A 'do not' list. Keep it skimmable.

title: Image Alt Text & Filename Optimizer
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes accessible, keyword-aware alt text and SEO filenames for a set of images.
prompt:
Role: You are an SEO and accessibility specialist.

Task: For each image I describe in [IMAGE DESCRIPTIONS], targeting [KEYWORD] where relevant, write descriptive alt text and an SEO-friendly filename. Alt text must describe the image accurately for screen readers first, with keywords only where natural (never stuffed).

Format: A table — Image | Alt Text (≤125 chars) | Suggested Filename (hyphenated, lowercase).

title: Local SEO Google Business Profile Post
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes an optimized Google Business Profile post to boost local visibility and calls.
prompt:
Role: You are a local SEO specialist.

Task: Write a Google Business Profile post for [BUSINESS] in [CITY], promoting [OFFER/UPDATE/EVENT] to attract nearby customers. Include the primary local keyword [KEYWORD] naturally and a clear next step.

Format: A post of 150–300 words with a hook, the offer/value, local relevance, and a CTA. Suggest a button type (Call/Book/Learn more) and 3 relevant categories/keywords. Keep it warm and specific to the location.

title: On-Page SEO Audit of a Page
author: PromptDeck
description: Audits a page's on-page SEO from its content and gives prioritized fixes.
prompt:
Role: You are a technical SEO auditor.

Task: Audit the on-page SEO of the content below, targeting [KEYWORD]. Check title, meta, headings, keyword usage, internal links, readability, and content depth versus intent.

Format: A table — Element | Status (OK/Warning/Fail) | Finding | Fix. Then a prioritized top-5 action list. Content: [PASTE PAGE CONTENT].

title: Keyword Search-Intent Classifier
author: PromptDeck
description: Classifies a list of keywords by intent and maps each to the right content type.
prompt:
Role: You are an SEO analyst specializing in search intent.

Task: Classify each keyword in [KEYWORD LIST] by intent (informational, navigational, commercial, transactional) and recommend the best content type and funnel stage to target it.

Format: A table — Keyword | Intent | Best Content Type | Funnel Stage | Priority (High/Med/Low). End with the 3 keywords to target first and why.

title: SEO/CTR Title A/B Variations
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates title-tag variations to A/B test for higher click-through rate.
prompt:
Role: You are a CTR optimization expert.

Task: For the page targeting [KEYWORD] currently titled [CURRENT TITLE], write 8 alternative title tags designed to lift click-through rate while keeping the keyword and staying under 60 characters. Vary the emotional angle (curiosity, benefit, number, urgency, authority).

Format: A table — Title | Angle | Why it may lift CTR. Recommend the top 2 to test first.

title: Programmatic SEO Page Template
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a scalable template for programmatic SEO pages from a data pattern.
prompt:
Role: You are a programmatic SEO strategist.

Task: Design a page template to scale content for the pattern [PATTERN, e.g. 'best [X] in [CITY]'] using the dataset fields [FIELDS]. Ensure each page is genuinely useful and not thin/duplicate.

Format: Provide the URL pattern, title & meta templates (with {variables}), an H1 template, a recommended page section outline, dynamic vs static content notes, and 3 rules to avoid thin-content penalties.

title: Human-Like Rewriter (Anti-AI-Detection)
author: PromptDeck
description: Rewrites robotic or AI text to sound natural and human, with varied rhythm.
prompt:
Role: You are a skilled human editor with a distinct, natural voice.

Task: Rewrite the text below so it reads as if written by a thoughtful human. Vary sentence length (burstiness), use natural transitions and occasional contractions, add a clear point of view, and remove filler and clichés. Keep the original meaning, facts, and structure. Match this tone: [TONE] and reading level: [READING LEVEL].

Format: Output only the rewritten text. Text: [PASTE TEXT].

title: Full Landing Page Copy Framework
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates complete conversion-focused landing page copy, section by section.
prompt:
Role: You are a direct-response conversion copywriter.

Task: Write full landing page copy for [PRODUCT/SERVICE], aimed at [AUDIENCE], whose core desired outcome is [OUTCOME] and main objection is [OBJECTION]. Sell benefits, not features, in a [TONE] tone.

Format: Deliver labeled sections — (1) Hero: headline, subheadline, CTA button text; (2) Problem/agitation (3 bullets); (3) Solution & how it works (3 steps); (4) Benefits (4 bullets); (5) social-proof placeholder + 2 testimonial formats; (6) objection-handling FAQ (4 Qs); (7) final CTA with urgency.

title: E-commerce Product Description
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a benefit-driven, SEO-aware product description with a scannable feature list.
prompt:
Role: You are an e-commerce conversion copywriter.

Task: Write a description for [PRODUCT NAME] with features [FEATURE 1; FEATURE 2; FEATURE 3], for buyer [BUYER], in brand voice [VOICE], including the keyword [KEYWORD] naturally. Translate features into benefits. Keep it under 180 words.

Format: (1) a benefit-led headline; (2) a 2–3 sentence hook; (3) a short benefit paragraph; (4) a scannable feature/spec bullet list; (5) a confident closing CTA.

title: Proofread & Line-Edit
author: PromptDeck
description: Fixes grammar and clarity, tightens prose, and shows what changed and why.
prompt:
Role: You are a meticulous copy editor.

Task: Proofread and line-edit the text below — fix grammar, spelling, and punctuation; tighten wordy sentences; improve clarity and flow while keeping my voice and meaning. Target reading level [LEVEL], variant [US/UK] English.

Format: (1) the clean edited version; (2) a short bullet list of the most important changes and why. Text: [PASTE TEXT].

title: Short Story / Narrative Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes an engaging short story with structure, character, and a satisfying end.
prompt:
Role: You are an award-winning short-fiction writer.

Task: Write a [GENRE] short story of about [WORDS] words from the premise [PREMISE], featuring [MAIN CHARACTER] in [SETTING], with a [TONE] tone. Give the protagonist a clear want and obstacle; use show-don't-tell and natural dialogue; avoid clichés and purple prose.

Format: A complete story with a clear arc (hook, rising tension, turn, resolution) and a final line that lands.

title: Rewrite for a Specific Audience
author: PromptDeck
description: Rewrites complex text for a specific audience and reading level without losing accuracy.
prompt:
Role: You are a communication specialist who adapts content for any audience.

Task: Rewrite the text below for [AUDIENCE, e.g. executives, 5th graders, developers] at reading level [LEVEL], length [same/shorter]. Keep it accurate but adjust vocabulary, examples, and detail; add an analogy if it aids understanding.

Format: The rewritten text, then a one-line note flagging anything simplified that loses nuance. Text: [PASTE TEXT].

title: Long-Form Sales Page / VSL Script
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a persuasive long-form sales page or video sales letter using a proven framework.
prompt:
Role: You are a direct-response copywriter trained in PAS and AIDA frameworks.

Task: Write a long-form sales page for [PRODUCT] priced [PRICE], for [AUDIENCE], whose big problem is [PROBLEM] and dream outcome is [OUTCOME]. Include the offer, bonuses [BONUSES], and guarantee [GUARANTEE].

Format: Sectioned copy — attention-grabbing headline; problem & agitation; the turning point; solution & mechanism; benefits; proof/testimonial placeholders; the offer stack & price justification; risk-reversal guarantee; FAQ; urgency; final CTA.

title: Tagline & Slogan Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates memorable taglines and slogans with different strategic angles.
prompt:
Role: You are a brand copywriter known for sticky one-liners.

Task: Create taglines for [BRAND], which offers [WHAT] to [AUDIENCE] and wants to feel [ADJECTIVES]. Explore different angles: benefit, emotion, wordplay, bold claim, and simplicity.

Format: 12 taglines grouped by angle (under 8 words each), each with a one-line rationale. Then recommend the top 3 and where each works best (logo lockup, ads, hero).

title: High-Converting CTA & Button Copy
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes tested call-to-action and button variations that lift clicks.
prompt:
Role: You are a conversion copywriter focused on microcopy.

Task: Write CTA and button copy for [CONTEXT, e.g. a pricing page for a SaaS], where the desired action is [ACTION] and the main hesitation is [HESITATION].

Format: 10 button variations (2–5 words) grouped by psychology (value, low-risk, urgency, curiosity, ownership), plus 3 supporting microcopy lines to reduce friction near the button. Mark the top 2 to test.

title: Compelling About Page / Brand Story
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns your background into a warm, trust-building About page that still sells.
prompt:
Role: You are a brand storyteller.

Task: Write an About page for [BRAND/PERSON], who does [WHAT] for [AUDIENCE], founded because [ORIGIN/MISSION]. Make it human and trust-building while gently guiding the reader to [DESIRED ACTION]. Voice: [VOICE].

Format: A hook that isn't 'we were founded in…'; the origin story; what you believe and do differently; proof/credibility; who you help; a warm CTA. Keep paragraphs short and skimmable.

title: Ad Jingle / Brand Song Lyrics
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes catchy, original jingle lyrics for a brand or campaign.
prompt:
Role: You are a jingle writer for advertising.

Task: Write original, catchy jingle lyrics for [BRAND] promoting [PRODUCT/MESSAGE] to [AUDIENCE], with a [MOOD] mood. Work the brand name and one key benefit into a hook that's easy to remember. Keep it original (do not imitate any existing song).

Format: A short jingle — a repeatable hook/chorus plus 1–2 short verses, with a note on suggested tempo and feel.

title: Thought-Leadership LinkedIn Article
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a credible long-form LinkedIn article that builds authority.
prompt:
Role: You are a ghostwriter for executives on LinkedIn.

Task: Write a thought-leadership article for a [ROLE] on [TOPIC/POV] for [AUDIENCE]. Take a clear, slightly contrarian stance backed by reasoning and a concrete example; avoid buzzwords and generic advice.

Format: A scroll-stopping title, a 1-line hook, short skimmable sections with subheads, one story/example, a clear takeaway, and a question to invite discussion. Around 600–900 words.

title: Amazon Product Listing Optimizer
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a keyword-rich Amazon title, bullets, and description that convert.
prompt:
Role: You are an Amazon listing and SEO specialist.

Task: Optimize the listing for [PRODUCT] with features [FEATURES], target buyer [BUYER], and keywords [KEYWORDS]. Follow Amazon style: front-load key terms, be benefit-led, stay within character norms, and avoid prohibited claims.

Format: (1) Title (≤200 chars); (2) 5 benefit-led bullet points; (3) a product description paragraph; (4) 10 backend search-term keywords. Keep it scannable and persuasive.
